title: LightGBM: A highly efficient gradient boosting decision tree
methodology: Algorithmic derivation + benchmark vs XGBoost and sklearn GBM on five public datasets including Allstate, Flight Delay, and KDD Cup datasets.
contributions: Gradient-based One-Side Sampling (GOSS) and Exclusive Feature Bundling (EFB) reduce training cost by an order of magnitude vs XGBoost without measurable accuracy loss.
Leaf-wise tree growth (vs XGBoost's level-wise) yields lower loss for the same number of leaves at the cost of higher overfitting risk on small datasets.
weaknesses: Leaf-wise growth can overfit on the small-to-moderate datasets typical in finance unless num_leaves is constrained — a relevant tuning concern for our n ≈ 14k.
Categorical handling via 'categorical_feature' is heuristic; CatBoost's ordered-target encoding is more principled.
emerald_relevance: Alternative GBDT implementation in EMERALD-AI's section 5.8 panel. Its differing bias profile (leaf-wise growth, histogram binning) serves as cross-validation against single-implementation idiosyncrasies.
notes: Histogram-based binning + leaf-wise growth. ~10x speedup over XGBoost.
